_comment: TQNN MCP Server — maps each employee (username, matching tqnn_mcp_users.json) to a DMM sub-credential pair generated via the appliance ACL console (tqnn_acl_manager.php 'generate' action). Dataset ACCESS is controlled there, NOT here. The optional 'dataset' field below just picks which dataset this pair uses BY DEFAULT for calls that don't explicitly specify one (e.g. tqnn_status/ping) — it must be one of the datasets that sub-credential is actually whitelisted for in the ACL console, or ping will 403. Omit it to fall back to .env's TQNN_DATASET. Employees with no entry below fall back to 'default'.
